concerns.text: The specification omits any requirement to perform the subgroup exploration described in the idea (step 7). The idea calls for summarizing inconsistency rates by source type and publication year, and applying Fisher’s exact test when a subgroup has ≥ 10 entries. No functional requirement or success criterion addresses this, constituting an under‑reach of the original research plan.

FR‑025 introduces a priori power analysis for the binomial prevalence test, which is not mentioned in the original idea. This adds a new methodological step that was not required to answer the research question, representing an over‑reach.

FR‑026 mandates Monte Carlo validation of each statistical test implementation. The idea’s methodology only specifies using SciPy and statsmodels for cross‑checking, without Monte Carlo simulation. This requirement exceeds the scope of the original proposal.

FR‑027 requires a bias assessment report flagging domains that exceed 30 % of the corpus. The idea does not request a formal bias adjustment or reporting beyond optional subgroup exploration, so this adds unnecessary scope.

FR‑028 demands a Quickstart guide enabling a novice to run the audit on 30 URLs within 30 minutes. While helpful, the idea does not specify any user‑documentation deliverable beyond a reproducible research package, making this an over‑reach.
